item.title: Finally! After 30 years I’ve completed and rebuild my Black Seas Barracuda
item.description:
It took me A LOT of work to gather all the bricks that were missing and complete this set. But I finally did it! I’ve build my LEGO Black Seas Barracuda (set 6285). More detailed (close-up) shots below this post Story time: When I was 11 years old, I saved money to buy this set. I think I’ve washed a hundred cars and it also took all my birthday money but it was all worth it. This was one of my favorite sets and it still is. I’ve always kept all my childhood LEGO sets in one big box. But all the sets together has been all mixed up and I never took the effort to sort it out (so were the bricks of this set). Long story short: I’ve moved this big LEGO box with me ever since I was a kid, and now I finally took the time to sort all the bricks for this ship and get the ones that were missing. A lot of bricks were missing..
Preparing with this checklist I found on internet. It helped to find back all the bricks of this set in my box of mixed LEGO. But also a lot of bricks were missing. It took me six orders to find all missing bricks and parts, using different brick stores online.
Close up of the lower deck
The original sails are all dirty, ragged and moldy. There was also one sail missing. After looking online for a replacement set, I decided to make sails myself out of black paper. Because even the replacement sets were very expensive.
New black sails made out of paper. I simply traced the original sails after ironing them. I carefully used a cut out template to make clean black sails. They are a bit stiffer than the originals but they work really well and look good.
Close up of the inside where the canons and cannonballs are stored. (auto focus wasn’t helping sorry for that)
I only had three of these coins left in my box of old LEGO. But I insisted to get everything complete. And with this set also came these coins. I had to look for them online and they cost me almost €1,- per coin. So that was a bad trade.
Close up shot of my favorite character Captain Red beard and his parrot.
Close up of the upper deck details
The figurehead on the front of the ship
This ship also comes with a rowing boat Building this set was harder than I thought it would be. I learned that old instructions are much worse to work with compared to new LEGO sets. There is no brick count for every step and a lot of steps are happening on a single page. You have to triple check every page before you continue.
The yellow details look great on this. I’m also happy with how the black sails turned out. Not like the original but it fits the color scheme perfectly.
Walking the plank. Not so fun fact: this set contains a lot of flags that are hard to find or super expensive. Especially the big pirate flag. Some of my flags have the clamps broke off. I still have to replace these one day.
Set info: Year Released: 1989 Weight: 2862g Item Dim.: 58 x 46.5 x 22 cm 845 Parts 8 Minifigures

item.title: This is not what you think it is
item.description: I see so many white supremacists using the punisher logo. Let me reiterate that if he were real he would hunt down and kill groups like the proud boys and the oath keepers. The punisher used to be one of my favorite heros. Now I feel like I cannot have any clothing or anything with the skull on it or I will be confused for a white supremacist.
item.dc:creator: SatanicOtaku

item.title: Well this sucks
item.description: After having a persistent cough for some time i went to my doctor who sent me to the hospital. After running multiple test the verdict is i have stage 4 hodgkin lymphoma. The prospects for hodgkin aren't that bad, about 80% change of recovery. Despite this i really doubt if i want to get treatment. I'm depressed af for so long and tired of this life but i also don't want to let down my parents. I think if i would die it will break them. I'm so lost what decision i should make.
item.dc:creator: Crispy4Skin

item.title: I just put down my best friend. I only hope he understands how much he was loved.
item.description:
He loved NASCAR.
He loved taking selfies.
He hated the stems of cilantro.
Cuddling was his favorite.
He wore hats. I loved those airplane ears.
Air vents were all his. Only his.
He loved his Valentines present.
But suddenly he didn't even want his favorite things.
We tried everything. He was put on so many meds to try to fix him. He looked miserable.
After so much torture and no progress, we made the hardest decision. I spent all night the night before cuddling him on the floor, watching his favorite shows.
item.dc:creator: FacePalmTrees

item.title: Just...yeah.
item.description: It's a secret that I've kept for the past 5 years. He couldn't be there that day, so his dad and I took Toby, a 13 year old Border Collie, in to be put down. He had an inoperable tumor in his intestines and his legs weren't functioning properly anymore. We had all decided that the best thing to do for our boy was to have him put down. His last moments have haunted me for years. I had never had a dog put down before. I didn't know that they were supposed to give him a sedative, and that they didn't. All I know is that he died screeching at the top of his lungs, muzzled, technician holding him down, just screaming one blood curdling scream after another. I have never heard such a horrible noise from an animal. He howled until his voice broke and then finally slipped away, still trying to cry. He died looking into my FIL's eyes, begging for help that would never come. While we were driving home with his body, we decided that our families didn't need to know. We would tell them that he slipped away peacefully, as though he'd fallen asleep. We agreed that we would tell everyone that we don't use that vet anymore because they're too expensive, not because of what had happened. He said that he never wanted to talk about it again. He felt like he'd killed him, he said, and he wanted to try to forget. I've carried the secret for years, and it's been so hard not having anyone to talk to about it. Last week, it finally came out. We were talking about taking our 20 year old cat in soon...he's not doing well and I'm terrified of that happening to him. After a moment of shock, my husband thanked me for telling him the truth and we cried together. He promised me that we would take extra precautions with the vet to make sure that they didn't hurt him like they hurt Toby. Having this off of my chest is such a relief. I had no idea how much better I would feel just not carrying that burden alone.
item.dc:creator: QuicklyAndWithPurpose

item.title: Reminder to all Australians.
item.dc:creator: lychgate

item.title: Some really fascinating/unusual places you might want to visit if you can.
item.description:
1) Meow Wolfs convergence station in Denver is fairly new, much larger than the Santa Fe or Las Vegas locations, and is easilly in my top 5 favorite places I've ever seen. It defies description really, just acres and acres of psychadelic fever dream maddness. just look it up because you simply can't comprehend the scale of it all. This cathedral is 4 stories tall and color changing.
The outside is pretty nondescript, If you don't know what you're going into its pretty astounding walking in.
One more pic for good measure. Seriously just go here.
2) The MIM, or museum of musical instruments in Scottsdale, AZ, is one of the most astonishing places I have ever been. The museums top floor is devoted to a display of every country on earth, divided by continent, and thier native instruments. What really makes this special is that at the entrance you are handed a bluetooth headset, which detects the country you are walking up to and plays a selection of music. Its so beautiful and overwhelming that you cant take it all in in a single visit.
The downstairs has a concert hall, a wing devoted to important artifacts and mementos of musical history, and a huge room where you can try out all sorts of exotic instruments (it can get cacophanous but in a fun way!) JUST GO HERE ITS GREAT!
3) Alright the Royal Tyrrel museum is in Alberta but I went once and it was amazing and I just wanted to include it because of the beautiful nodosaur "mummy" you see below, the best preserved dinosaur ever found. I am a sucker for top notch natural history and this place is fantastic.
4) Having lived near the DC area for some years of course I have a fondness for the Smithsonian museums but you know what beats the pants off the air and space museum? The National Museum of the United States Airforce in Dayton, Ohio. Seriously, if you're in Ohio, you probably have a lot of time on your hands, so just go here.
As a lover of all things flight, this place was like my graceland. 5 dizzyingly massive airplane hangers containing some of the most significant machines in aeronautic history, including a very realistic space shuttle landing sim (I did terribly. You should go and try to do better than I.)
5) Mesa Verde National park in the southwest corner of Colorado- thousand year old cliff dwellings, they were massive complexes housing 150 people, and there are dozens of seperate versions of this all along the canyon walls, it's staggeringly beautiful.
6) The banana museum in Mecca, California! More scales than you can shake a stick at. Honestly it was pretty silly, I only included it for Imgur posterity.
7) The Cryptozoology museum in Portland, Maine! This place is pretty silly too, but in a fun way. Lots of delightfully cheesy displays of mythical creatures and random stuffed real animals. If you mess up and accidentally go to Portland *Oregon*, there's a place called Paxton Gate that's like a hipster taxidermy shop that'll do ya in good stead.
I would like the mothman as halloween yard decor, please and thank you.
8) The City Museum in St. Louis, Missouri! This place is billed as a kids museum but it's a blast for adults too. I mean, who doesn't want to monkey around a drastically modified old shoe factory and pretend your good at parkour? It's a blasty blast!
The inside is really beautiful too.
9) TIS THE SEASON for Great Sand Dunes national park! This place is so magical, in the late spring/early summer the snowpack held in the mountains about these 1000ft high dunes melts, forming a seasonal shallow beach scene. Its incredible beautiful and incredibly fun, as the ambient temperature is very cool due to altitude, but the sand/water is like 80 degrees.
10) I just threw this one in as a shoutout to the people that have responsibly fun, non-depressing animal parks. sitting beneath Picacho peak (also a neat place to hike) north of Tucson Arizona is this fun and kitschy petting zoo with plenty of well cared for animals- goats and mini donkies and deer and giant rabbits and a lorikeet enclosure, its super fun.
Do not get close to the ostriches tho, they peck your head to make you drop the animal feed.
11) Another fabulous National Park that's pretty far flung, New Mexicos White Sands ethereal beauty never leaves you. Seriously, I dream about this place. The white dunes are made of miles and miles of powdery gypsum crystal.
The animals there have evolved to be white, which is just so COOL!
12) I see a lot of jokes about people wishing Yellowstone would just go off already, and here at Ashfall State Park in Nebraska you can see the sobering effects of just that occurrence!
Animals from the Miocene like ancestral horses and rhinos and the like, perfectly preserved under a foot of fossilized ash. Cool, if not a bit creepy.
13) THE CORN PALACE OF MITCHELL SOUTH DAKOTA!!! Updated yearly with different corn and wheat made mural all over the building.
14) Chincoteague Island off the coast of Virginia- Did anyone else read Marguerite Henrys classic novel Misty of Chincoteague? Well Behold! They stuffed Misty and stuck her in a tacky little museum. I feel like she should be called Dusty now. But Chincoteague and neighboring Assateague island are both still wonderful nature preserves for the wild ponies that have been there for hundreds of years, and worth a visit.
15) I have traveled extensively thanks to having worked for airlines, and I will say as far flung as Chena hot springs and Ice museum in Fairbanks Alaska is, it is SO worth seeing. Its immensely popular for Northern Lights Tourists.
Makes me think the White Witch from Narnia was a real classy broad.
16) Oh come on, WE LOVE DOLLY! And she may not officially be the owner here anymore, but its still and fun and wholesome and they treat their workers well (lookin' at you Disneyland) and frankly there are worse places to visit than Pidgeon Forge in Tennesee (lookin' at you, Gatlinburg). Seriously tho, Dollywood is a charming novelty theme park and 100% worth it if you're in the area.
and in season its gorgeous!
17) I thought a nice bookend to Dolly would be this awesome gal here, Madame Ganna Walska, and her fantastical botanical Gardens called Lotusland near Santa Barbara, California. She was famous opera singer in her heyday, and 4 of her 6 husbands were very wealthy, so she built this gorgeous, sprawling garden of many climate zones.
couldn't decide on a single picture so I just decided to screen cap the google image page for it. But trust me, its amazing.
18) Any Frank Gehry fans in the house? This is the Weisman Art museum on the grounds of The University of Minnesota, and it is trippy.
The modern art within is just as fun as the crazy external. This is just a pic I pulled off google, none of my pics were that good. Still tho, go visit if you have some hours to kill in Minneapolis.
19) Seattle is one of my favorite cities- so much to feature. But If you haven't yet been to Chihuly Garden and Glass, GO, GO NOW, DON'T DELAY! It's just so beautiful, and Dale Chihuly has an intersting story and such a talented team of artists working with him, worth a wikipedia read if you can't make it to Seattle.
WE HAVE COME TO THE END OF THE LIST OF PLACES I THOUGHT WERE NEAT AND WANTED YOU ALL TO KNOW ABOUT. Thanks for looking, and if this picks up any steam I can do an international version. PLEASE COMMENT WITH PLACES YOU THINK ARE NEAT THAT PEOPLE MIGHT NOT KNOW ABOUT!
item.dc:creator: noproblllama
